purpose: Preserve a coherent, evidence-first visual and information system for complex repository README work.

layout.content_width: GitHub default content width; verify a narrow viewport too.
layout.hierarchy: One H1, action-oriented H2 sections, short lead paragraphs, tables only for repeated choices, and release evidence in a managed block.
layout.theme: Evidence-first technical editorial: restrained, readable, and project-native rather than dashboard-like.

component_rules: Use Markdown for claims, commands, links, and accessibility text.
Do not add counters, trackers, profile widgets, remote fonts, or unverified badges for decoration.
Use PNG/JPG/WebP for generated raster composition; retain SVG or Mermaid only when editable structure materially helps.
Re-use approved colors, whitespace, card-like geometry, and visual hierarchy before adding a new treatment.

architecture_structure.role: Editable and reviewable exact structural reference.
architecture_structure.regenerate_when: The architecture graph or owner relationships change materially.

maintenance.complexity_threshold: Whole README, six or more headings, three or more top-level sections changed, or more than one visual changed.
required_checks: design-system manifest update when visual decisions change

user_facing_explanation_policy.route: Evaluate every changed main user-facing explanation with github-readme-presentation. Use README mode for complex reader journeys and Audit mode otherwise.
